title: Galene and the Unlit Room
logline: Galene descends into Aithon and learns the dark was only unremembered light.
story: Galene, who carried serene calm, went looking for Aithon, who was the burning, blazing one. Everyone warned that Aithon could not be reached; the way down was made of everything Galene had refused to remember. So instead of fighting the dark, Galene sat in it and listened — and the dark, for the first time, was not afraid of being seen. When Galene rose, nothing outside had changed, and everything had. The darkness had not been an enemy; it was only a room with the light switched off, waiting for someone unhurried enough to find the switch within.
moral: The darkness is not the absence of light, but light that has not yet been remembered.
attribution: a Pagarek parable, from Project Genesis
